title: Electronic trade processing system and method
abstract: In one implementation, a computer-implemented method is executed on a computing device and includes: receiving a deallocation notice from a market participant concerning assets within a trading account; initiating an auto-deallocation period; determining if there is outstanding trade activity within the trading account during the auto-deallocation period; and if there is no outstanding trade activity within the trading account during the auto-deallocation period, transferring at least a portion of the assets within the trading account to the market participant.
claims:
Claims What Is Claimed Is: 1. A computer-implemented method, executed on a computing device, comprising: receiving a deallocation notice from a market participant concerning assets within a trading account; initiating an auto-deallocation period; determining if there is outstanding trade activity within the trading account during the auto-deallocation period; and if there is no outstanding trade activity within the trading account during the auto-deallocation period, transferring at least a portion of the assets within the trading account to the market participant. 2. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 wherein the outstanding trade activity includes one or more of: previously-received unsettled trade activity; and newly-received unsettled trade activity. 3. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 further comprising: if there is outstanding trade activity within the trading account during the auto-deallocation period, rejecting the deallocation notice. 4. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 further comprising: if there is outstanding trade activity within the trading account during the auto-deallocation period, attempting to settle the outstanding trade activity during the auto-deallocation period. 5. The computer-implemented method of claim 4 further comprising: if the outstanding trade activity cannot be settled due to inaction by the trading platform, transferring at least a portion of the assets within the trading account to the market participant. 6. The computer-implemented method of claim 4 further comprising: if the outstanding trade activity cannot be settled due to inaction by the market participant, rejecting the deallocation notice. 7. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 wherein the assets within the trading account are general collateral assets. 8. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 wherein the trading account is a trading platform account managed by a third party platform and associated with a trading platform. 9. The computer-implemented method of claim 8 further comprising one or more of: enabling the market participant to review the content of the trading platform account; and enabling the trading platform to review the content of the trading platform account. 10. The computer-implemented method of claim 8 wherein the third party platform includes: a management entity; and a storage entity. 11. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 wherein the assets include one or more of: a fiat currency; and a cryptocurrency. 12. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 further comprising: notifying a trading platform associated with the trading account of the deallocation notice. 13. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 further comprising: allowing the trading platform to authorize the transfer of at least a portion of the assets within the trading account to the market participant during the auto-deallocation period. 14. A computer program product residing on a computer readable medium having a plurality of instructions stored thereon which, when executed by a processor, cause the processor to perform operations comprising: receiving a deallocation notice from a market participant concerning assets within a trading account; initiating an auto-deallocation period; determining if there is outstanding trade activity within the trading account during the auto-deallocation period; and if there is no outstanding trade activity within the trading account during the auto-deallocation period, transferring at least a portion of the assets within the trading account to the market participant. 15.  previously-received unsettled trade activity; and newly-received unsettled trade activity. 16.
